product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Flexible multilingual corporate language programs for Luxembourg companies that want tailored delivery, reporting and scheduling.
product_summary.target_user: Luxembourg employers and HR teams that need language training for staff in one or more languages.
product_summary.core_pain: Companies need language training that fits roles, schedules and internal reporting without becoming a generic classroom offer.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Improved employee language ability with HR-visible tracking and possible training-aid support.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The product is a classic employer-funded training service and HR/L&D is the most likely procurement owner.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Business-unit leaders and company management can sponsor team language budgets.
The service may be delivered through external training or implementation partners.

budget_owner.reason: The buyer motion is most naturally owned by HR/L&D in a corporate setting.

buyer_influencers.reason: HR typically cares about participation, reporting and training logistics.
Managers influence whether the language program is useful for team execution.
In smaller firms, leadership may decide on training spend directly.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: INFPC co-financing of 15-35% is relevant to this product.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: General Luxembourg company training co-financing exists and can reach 15% with a 35% uplift for certain employees. Product-level eligibility still depends on the exact plan and provider setup.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Berlitz is global, Prolingua is local and freelancers do not provide structured tracking.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Berlitz and Prolingua clearly offer corporate language services. The 'freelancers do not provide structured tracking' part is not directly verified and should not be treated as fact.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Ministry of Education certified implies INFPC eligibility.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No public source reviewed confirms this provider-specific certification route.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: LMS analytics, multilingual delivery and flexible scheduling together create a unique edge.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The public market contains similar flexibility and tailored delivery; the exact edge is not externally established.

funding_bodies.name: INFPC
funding_bodies.relevance: Administers Luxembourg co-financing for continuing vocational training.

funding_bodies.name: Guichet.lu
funding_bodies.relevance: Publishes official instructions and eligibility rules for employer training co-financing.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Luxembourg company training co-financing can reach 15% of annual investment and 35% for eligible wage costs.

subsidies_or_funding.name: Company training co-financing
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: General aid exists, but the exact eligibility of this corporate language service requires validation.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Prolingua
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Offers tailor-made courses in companies, at premises or remotely, with specialist language options.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: inlingua Luxembourg
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Offers company and individual language courses with online and in-person delivery.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Berlitz Luxembourg
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Provides corporate language solutions for international teams across many languages.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The product uses training-aid wording, not formal regulated-practice or accreditation claims.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: Core delivery does not depend on formal external approval.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Funding and certification claims should be checked externally before being used publicly.

proof_stack.reason: A corporate language service must win against generic local competitors and freelancers.
HR buyers usually want proof that tracking and flexibility improve completion or productivity.
Language improvement remains an important intermediate proof signal.

proof_burden.reason: The service is not regulated, but it still needs commercial proof and evidence that its tracking layer adds value.

dependency_types.reason: LMS tracking, progress reporting and session recordings require privacy review.
The proposition depends on Luxembourg-specific training aid and employer expectations.
The INFPC angle must be validated per client and plan.
Custom language programs need a reliable bench of teachers across languages and levels.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The product is broad but confined to a single market and a standard corporate-training motion.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Outside Luxembourg, training-aid logic and buyer expectations would change materially.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The product category fits general employer training aid, but the exact course configuration needs validation.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No direct public conflict was found for the category itself.

market_regulatory_context.reason: The product is a standard corporate training service with some funding-rule checks.

claim_language_risk.reason: The certification and funding language should be softened until validated.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: Delivery itself does not rely on formal authority; only the funding angle does.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Several established corporate language providers already offer tailored and online/hybrid delivery.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: No numeric market claim is central to this product card.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: Eligibility is plausible but not proven for the exact product configuration.

positioning_risk.reason: The product can easily look like a generic language school offer unless the reporting and tracking layer is demonstrated.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Ministry of Education certified implies INFPC eligible.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: No public source reviewed confirms that this specific certification route exists or that it automatically grants eligibility.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Berlitz, Prolingua and freelancers are the full substitute set.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The reviewed market evidence shows substitutes, but it is not an exhaustive census of the Luxembourg market.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Validate any Ministry-of-Education or similar certification language before using it publicly.
Make sure the reporting layer is strong enough to justify the premium positioning.
Check whether each client setup is actually eligible for training co-financing.

sources.title: Aides à la formation professionnelle continue en entreprise
Institut national pour le développement de la formation professionnelle continue (INFPC)
Tailor-made courses in your company, at Prolingua's premises or at a distance
Cours de langues pros & particuliers Luxembourg
